source: Amani/Sheikh-Hosseini et al., 'Demographic changes of nomadic communities in Iran (1956-2008)', Asian Population Studies 6(3), 2010, DOI 10.1080/17441730.2010.512764 (table 'Foreign researcher estimates of the nomad population in Iran, 1884-1908', cited via secondary aggregation, primary paywalled)

notes: 'A century ago, the nomadic population was prominent in the demographic structure of Iran, possibly constituting about 25 to 30 per cent of the total population' -- narrative estimate, not a census figure

Library of Congress Country Study estimate: 'nomads totaled 1.8 million' (1986); broader combined nomadic+settled tribally-organized population estimated at ~4 million -- differs from SCI's own dedicated Nomad Census methodology (see 1987 row), reflecting different definitional scope (self-identified tribal affiliation vs. actively-migrating pastoral household)
First dedicated Statistical Centre of Iran (SCI) Census of Nomadic (Ashayeri) Population -- 180,223 households, grouped into 96 tribes (il) and 547 independent clans (tayefeh-ye mostaqell)
source: Statistical Centre of Iran 1987 Nomad Census

notes: Second SCI Nomad Census -- population continued increasing trend from 1987 before the subsequent decline recorded in 2008
source: Asian Population Studies 6(3) 2010 (secondary citation; exact census year for the 'second census' not independently confirmed by this agent -- flagged as approximate)

notes: General Population & Housing Census 'unsettled' population category (Iran census terminology) -- NOT the same measure as the dedicated Nomad Census; this is a census-day snapshot of persons enumerated while migrating/outside settled villages, structurally undercounting true pastoral-nomadic population relative to the dedicated Ashayeri census. Provincial breakdown available (all 31 ostans).

notes: Third SCI Nomad Census -- decline of ~117,000 from the second census, attributed largely to settlement/urban migration. Annual economic output attributed to nomadic tribes per SCI 2008: ~160,000 sq. m of carpets, ~330,000 tonnes of milk, ~13,000 tonnes of wool.
source: Statistical Centre of Iran 2008 Nomad Census
